# Transcrição: 26-Custom Audience- Website Visitors 

- **Fonte:** 26-Custom Audience- Website Visitors 
- **Duração:** 04:27
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

## [00:00 - 03:04]

So let's take a look at how you can create custom audiences from website visitors. So initially you need to go to your business manager or ads manager and you need to navigate to the audiences tab. Once you've done that you're going to be redirected to this screen here and you just need to click create audience custom audience and then it's going to show up with all these options. So if you want to create a custom audience based on website traffic or website visitors you need to click on this one. So this is going to be able to use data captured on your Facebook pixel to reach out to people and to retarget people who took certain actions or went to certain pages. So this is really really powerful. So we're going to click on here. It's going to pop up with this menu and then it's going to say the initial prompt is just retargeting all website visitors in the past 30 days. So here you actually have an ability to retarget people up until the last 180 days but typically for these custom audiences you want to make sure that you're retargeting people who are of the highest intent. So someone who has visited your website 180 days ago probably isn't even going to remember who you are or that your brand even exists. So you want to make sure that when you're creating these custom audiences you're using people who actually know who your brand is. So here like I mentioned the default is all website visitors but you can get really really in depth here. So what you can get into here is you can look at people who visited certain web pages. So if we want to do that you can just say okay they visited a certain URL and let's say you want to retarget people who added to cart and on your cart page your URL is my favorite shopifystore.com slash cart. So you know everyone who visited the cart page triggered or had a URL that triggered cart so you would add cart in there. And this is really easy it makes it very very simple so if you want to retarget by a specific catalog or a collection or let's say you only want to retarget people who checked out pants on your e-commerce store or people who checked out socks you just add that or you can add more as well. So let's say you want pants and socks then you just do that. So any URL that contains socks or pants is going to be included in this custom audience. Now beyond that you can also create custom audiences based on the fact that the URL does not contain certain words. So let's say you only want you want to retarget every other product except socks maybe because there's a really low average order value with socks or it's really low products low end products. This is an example of how you can create a custom audience for those types of people as well. And the final one here is equal so you can retarget people based on a specific URL as well. So if you only want to retarget people who hit a certain URL through your funnel you can do that here as well. Now beyond that you can also create custom audiences based on the amount of time a visitor spent on your site. So this is actually really

## [03:04 - 04:27]

powerful right and you can look at the top 25 percent the top 10 percent or the top 5 percent and then you can break it down based on how long ago. Again this is going to be maxed out at 180 days. So this can be really really powerful to see who your top website visitors are and how to retarget them and how to create custom audiences based on those people. Now beyond that you can also use other custom audiences based on events on your page. So page view, view content you can retarget people who have just added to cart. So this would be people who have abandoned their cart. And what you can also do here is exclude people. So here what you might want to do is exclude people who have purchased. So you would create add to cart and then you would exclude people who have purchased in the last 30 days. So this is making sure that you're not retargeting those people who have actually purchased. You're only retargeting the people who have not purchased but have added to cart. So these are people who are very high intent. And then as the last step here you want to name your custom audience. So here we might go with something like add to cart 30 days but no purchase. You might want to be a little bit more concise than that but that's essentially how you create a custom audience based on people who have visited your website.
